skill.name: fal-image-edit
skill.description: Guidance for planning AI image edits, including style transfer and object removal workflows.
skill.summary: Plan AI-powered image edits with Fal-oriented guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs and weak cryptographic algorithms in SKILL.md. Review found the URLs are documentation links, and the weak-crypto matches are false positives in prose about AI image editing. No executable code, prompt injection, credential handling, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22 are source repository references. They are not executable network requests and do not transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations contain plain GitHub documentation links. There is no script, command, or API call that could use these URLs to send data.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20 are prose text about AI-powered image editing. No cryptographic function or algorithm use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are descriptive Markdown and front matter. They contain no code path, library call, key material, or cryptographic operation.

content.user_title: Edit Images with Fal AI Guidance
content.value_statement: Image editing prompts often fail when goals, source images, and constraints are vague. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure Fal-oriented edit requests for style transfer and object removal.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use the skill for AI-powered image editing tasks.
Frames workflows for style transfer requests.
Frames workflows for object removal requests.
Provides general guidance and patterns for image editing prompts.
Points users to the upstream source repository for more information.
limitations: Does not include executable code or an API integration.
Does not document model parameters, authentication, or deployment steps.
Does not guarantee edit quality without clear source images and constraints.
Does not provide safety policy guidance for restricted image content.

use_cases.title: Prepare Product Image Edits
use_cases.description: Create a concise editing brief for removing distractions or applying a consistent visual style.

use_cases.title: Refine Creative Concepts
use_cases.description: Turn a loose visual idea into an image edit request with style and composition constraints.

use_cases.title: Document Image Edit Requests
use_cases.description: Write repeatable instructions for AI-assisted image edits in a team workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Object Removal
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple cleanup edit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a clear Fal image edit request to remove an unwanted object from this image while preserving the background.

prompt_templates.title: Style Transfer Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a consistent visual style.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image edit brief for applying a new style to my source image. Include the desired look, protected details, and output expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Campaign Image Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a batch of AI image edits for campaign assets. Include object removal rules, style consistency checks, and review criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Edit Specification
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with constraints and quality controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a detailed Fal-oriented image edit specification with source image notes, transformation goals, negative constraints, and acceptance criteria.

output_examples.input: Remove a distracting sign from a storefront photo.
output_examples.output: A structured edit brief that names the object to remove, protects the storefront edges, preserves lighting, and defines review checks.

output_examples.input: Make a portrait look like a clean editorial image.
output_examples.output: A style transfer request that describes the target aesthetic, keeps facial identity unchanged, and sets limits on background changes.

output_examples.input: Prepare a repeatable workflow for product image edits.
output: A short intake checklist for source images.
A review checklist for consistency and artifacts.

best_practices: Describe the source image, edit goal, protected regions, and output format before requesting an edit.
Separate style goals from object removal goals so the model can preserve important details.
Review edited images for artifacts, identity changes, and unintended content changes.
anti_patterns: Do not request broad edits without naming what must remain unchanged.
Do not assume the skill includes Fal API credentials or execution code.
Do not use low-resolution or ambiguous source images when precise edits matter.

faq.question: Does this skill run Fal image editing models directly?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and prompt patterns, not executable API code.

faq.question: Can it help with object removal?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes object removal as a supported image editing workflow.

faq.question: Can it help with style transfer?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help structure requests that apply a desired style to an image.

faq.question: Does it include authentication instructions?
faq.answer: No. The available file does not include credential, API key, or deployment guidance.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation only and no executable risky behavior.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
